violations.finding: Contains inline bash implementation logic for output path generation: if/elif conditionals, env var guards, mkdir, and echo with computed paths. This is real executable logic (not an example), hardcoded with /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ and $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex/ path construction.
violations.recommendation: Extract to scripts/codex-output-path.sh accepting CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID and CODEX_OUT_ROLE as parameters, echoing the resolved output path. Command file references the script by name.

violations.finding: Contains inline bash execution block for review mode: runs 'codex review' with hardcoded model and reasoning_effort flags, piping to tee with path constructed from env vars. This is implementation logic, not an illustrative example.
violations.recommendation: Extract to scripts/codex-run.sh with mode parameter (review|exec), model, reasoning_effort, output_path as arguments.

violations.finding: Contains two inline bash execution blocks for exec mode (default and --model variants): runs 'codex exec' with hardcoded model/reasoning_effort flags and branched output paths. Duplicate logic with minor variation between the two blocks.
violations.recommendation: Merge with review mode extraction into scripts/codex-run.sh. The command file should invoke the script with appropriate mode/model arguments.

standards_passed: no-hardcoded-values (policies/tool-policy.v1.json — JSON policy file, standard not applicable)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Skill(codex) invocation triggered the /codex command machinery itself — the skill interpreted the audit prompt as a /codex exec invocation and displayed the codex.md command file content instead of returning structured CODEX_FEEDBACK. This is a known failure mode when calling the codex skill from within the codex command's own audit context.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted — 5 adversarial questions answered: (1) false positive check on Standard 1: confirmed real violations — bash blocks at lines 87-106, 113-119, 126-141 contain if/else logic, env var guards, mkdir, and real codex CLI invocations piped to tee; not labeled as examples; standard criteria clearly met; (2) missed violations check on policies/tool-policy.v1.json: JSON config/policy file — Standards 1/2/3/7/8/9 do not apply to declarative config; Standard 6 clean (no Chinese chars); Standard 11 clean (no conflict markers); Standard 10 clean (no script references); (3) severity calibration: critical is correct for all three inline-code violations per the guidelines which state all violations are critical; (4) scope check: Standard 6 unconditional scan of all agents/ and commands/ dirs completed — no Chinese chars found in any dir; all other standards correctly scoped to --changed-files only; (5) Standard 8 dismissal confirmed — calling protocol rules 1-4 are binding directives with specific enumerated requirements, not marketing language or narrative prose.
